assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable.
The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable, so the issue is framed by the IFRS 9 hedged item guidance for recognised items and intragroup positions.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of them.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model, including for foreign currency risk and highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
